rulings.Case Title: BOB WRIGHT VS. JAMES VARGHESE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT JAMES VARGHESE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to allege ultimate facts to support his causes of action. Amendment will not cure this defect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO JACOBO VS. NAVIGATORS INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANTs NAVIGATORS INSURANCE COMPANY, NAVIGATORS SPECIALITY INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. On a demurrer, the Court will only look at the face of the complaint and the facts alleged are deemed to be true. Plaintiff, therefore, sufficiently alleges he is an assignee of Scott Peters and the Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute the alleged causes of action. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the requested documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. NORTHERN TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, A FEDERALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 4, Defendants' Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part. The depositions of principal witnesses (Bell, Cameron and Pina) will each be limited to three days. The depositions of Bruce Webb and Jean Ulatowski) will each be limited to one half day of testimony. The deposition of Allan Cave will be limited to two days. The depositions of all other witnesses duly and timely noticed will each be limited to one day, unless extended by mutual agreement of the parties or pursuant to a further discovery order of the court. The further depositions of Eduardo Chavez, Ellen Foster, Xochitl Benson and Kate Van Glytenbeek will each be limited to 2.5 hours of testimony. The deposition of Amyre Coleman may be taken for no more than 2 hours.

rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. NORTHERN TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, A FEDERALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Northern Trust'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 4, PART TWO OF TENTATIVE RULING - Defendant Northern Trust'S Motion For Protective Order - The deposition of Martha Costello may be taken for no more than 3 hours. Plaintiff will not seek, through discovery, to obtain additional information regarding Rachel Canin's complaint against Kevin Rochford. In all other respects, the motion is denied. Neither party shall recover its fees in connection with this motion. Counsel for the plaintiff shall prepare an appropriate order reflecting this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNI FORD VS. TRAVEL INSURED INTERNATIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT ON CALL INTERNATIONAL INSURANCE AGENCY LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend as to the third and fifth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and sixth causes of action. On a demurrer, the Court will only look at the face of the complaint and the facts alleged are deemed to be true. Plaintiff has failed to allege his standing as successor in interest. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts to demonstrate his standing to bring the third and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff has failed to state facts sufficient to constitute claims for negligent infliction of emotional distress and breach of contract. Based on the nature of the allegations, it is unlikely leave to amend can cure these defects. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Cross-Complainants Cantrell have pled facts sufficient to allege each cause of action. A contract is not required to assert a quantum meruit claim. (CACI no. 371.) Whether Plaintiff was responsible under the contract is a question of fact, not subject to demurrer. (See First Am. Cross-Complaint, 6, Exhibit A, 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VISITACTION VALLEY COMMUNITY CENTER, A CALIFORNIA VS. MIESHA BELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Appeal Of The Order Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner And Failure To Post
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 8, RESPONDENT MIESHA BELL's Motion To Dismiss The Appeal Of The Order Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner And Failure To Post. Denied. The appeal is deemed timely filed because it was timely presented for filing and failure to include a judicial council form is not a sufficient basis to refuse to file a document which has a jurisdictional deadline. The bond requirement is waived pursuant to the Court's ruling on Defendant's Motion for Relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VISITACTION VALLEY COMMUNITY CENTER, A CALIFORNIA VS. MIESHA BELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Relief From Labor Code Section 98.2 (B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012, Line 8, APPELLANT VISITACTION VALLEY COMMUNITY CENTER's Motion For Relief From Labor Code Section 98.2 (B). Granted. Defendant has shown that it is unable to post the bond or obtain sufficient sureties. Under the factual circumstances presented here, waiving the bond is appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
